{{Infobox group
| name = Great Elders
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[Great Elders Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The Great [[Elders]] are a group of immensely powerful, eternal, and alien beings known to some as [[Class One Fiends]]. Though they are often viewed as malicious or even actively evil, many consider them to be strange benefactors whose alien natures and incomprehensible goals make their presence inherently dangerous to humanity. They possess abilities far beyond human control, often manifesting their power through specific containers rather than directly. While they frequently pursue conflicting individual objectives—such as Ach’magut seeking knowledge, [[Nakothi]] pursuing the balance of [[life]] and death, [[Urg’naut]] seeking annihilation, [[Tharlos]] desiring change, and [[Kthanikahr]] pursuing worms—they share a collective ultimate goal: to escape the world they view as a prison by passing through a [[crack in the sky]]. Historically, the [[Elders]] were defeated roughly fifteen hundred years ago during an era of chaos, but they do not die permanently; instead, they enter a state of sleep from which they can eventually awaken. Various factions interact with them in different ways: the [[Blackwatch]] captures and examines them, while a group called the [[Sleepless]] seeks to communicate with them to gain wisdom for mankind. One notable [[Elder]], [[Kelarac]] (the Collector of Souls), was a legendary bargainer who traded wishes for items like blood, sanity, or artifacts. Other known [[Elders]] include [[Othaghor]] and the [[Dead Mother]]. Despite their ability to be killed when in human form, they remain a constant threat, often attempting to corrupt human leaders to gain control over the species.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The only details known of the [[Great Elders]] come from their greatest opponent.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Emperor]] designed techniques that work against the spawn of certain [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The [[Sleepless]] aim to communicate with the [[Great Elders]] to benefit from their wisdom.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c22">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Most [[Great Elders]] view themselves as benefactors.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Great Elders]] use their powers through containers rather than directly.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c15">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[The Emperor]] has living memories of the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c16">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The [[Great Elders]] are subjects of horror stories told by [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c18">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|According to The [[Sleepless]], the [[Great Elders]] are not dead but merely sleeping.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c27">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Great [[Elders]] are powerful, immortal entities that utilize the [[void]] for both communication and transportation. They possess broad influence through a global network of devices designed to amplify [[Intent]], and they can even core and replace individuals. While [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] may use the [[Optasia]] as a conduit to respond to them, these moving entities often pursue conflicting goals and set the stage for specific events. Because humanity requires someone capable of negotiating with them on their own terms, [[Calder]] takes their wishes into consideration, noting that they might even task [[Jerri]] with the death of [[Shera]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Elders]] are powerful entities that [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] could respond to using the [[Optasia]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Elders]] have broad power and access to a global net of devices that amplify [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Elders]] often have conflicting goals.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Humanity needs someone who can deal with the [[Great Elders]] on their terms.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Great Elders]] might task [[Jerri]] with killing [[Shera]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Great Elders]] are entities that move.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] considers the wishes of the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Great Elders]] are a group that the [[Optasia]] acts as a conduit to.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Great Elders]] are immortals that can be negotiated with.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Great Elders]] can core a person and replace them.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Great Elders]] set the stage for certain events.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Great Elders]] use the [[void]] for transportation and communication.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c24">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The Great [[Elders]] are powerful entities that were defeated nearly fifteen hundred years ago and once held [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] in service. While legends suggest there are six such beings, they remain a subject that [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] avoids discussing. Unlike humans, these entities do not truly die but can instead gather themselves to potentially wake from death. Although [[Jorin]] has unraveled more of their ancient secrets than any other human, the Great [[Elders]] maintain a desire to destroy the [[Empire]]. Through the use of the [[Optasia]], it is possible to understand and negotiate with them on an equal level.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Jorin]] has unraveled more ancient secrets of the [[Great Elders]] than any other human.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c4">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|There are six other [[Great Elders]] according to old legends.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c9">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Great Elders]] are a group that [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] avoids talking about.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c15">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Optasia]] allows for the understanding and negotiation of [[Great Elders]] on an equal level.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Great Elders]] are powerful entities that were defeated almost fifteen hundred years ago.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c16">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Great Elders]] do not die like humans; they can gather themselves and potentially wake from death.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Great Elders]] are beings that [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] once served.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c18">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Great Elders]] want to destroy the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c21">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Historically involved in the [[Elder War]], the four remaining Great [[Elders]] are entities so feared that superstitions exist regarding naming them directly. Though they were once fought by [[Jorin]] and [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]], they have recently begun to move, leaving a [[crack in the sky]] as they awaken from their imprisonment. While security around them has been weak, they may be watching closely and possess strong servants to aid their cause. The Great [[Elders]] now seek to pass through the skyward crack to ascend beyond this world, potentially using human vessels to achieve their goal. Because of these ambitions, [[Bliss]] works to protect people from them, as it is vital that the Great [[Elders]] are kept sealed within their prison.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">17</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The false Emperor would deliver people to the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c1">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] are involved in the upcoming meeting.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Shera]] reports that the [[Great Elders]] are moving.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[The Emperor]] and [[Jorin]] had fought the [[Great Elders]] together.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|There are superstitions about naming [[Great Elders]] directly.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c8">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The security on the [[Great Elders]] has been weak.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c9">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] may be watching the place closely.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] left a [[crack in the sky]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c11">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Bliss]] seeks to protect people from the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c13">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] want to pass through the [[crack in the sky]] to ascend beyond this world.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] may seek human bodies or vessels.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] were involved in the [[Elder War]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Great Elders]] possess strong servants.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] need to be kept sealed.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|There are four remaining [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] are awake.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Great Elders]] are attempting to leave the world in which they are imprisoned.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The Great [[Elders]] are true enemies that exist in a manner far removed from human understanding. These rising entities are currently gathering their strength and planning an unknown scheme, potentially seeking to force a unification or take over the world by possessing a single person on the throne. Their ultimate goal is to escape the world, an ambition they pursued by cracking open the sky as an act of opportunity. While their involvement suggests that no good ending is possible and they possess the power to kill every major leader in the [[Empire]], they are vulnerable when in human form. To maintain security against them, the [[Guilds]] typically cooperate and retain the ability to oppose the Great [[Elders]] during times of emergency.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">16</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] are the true enemies.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c2">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] might be trying to force unification.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] do not live as humans understand it.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c3">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Great Elders]] are capable of killing every major leader in the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c4">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] are planning something.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The involvement of [[Great Elders]] means there will be no good ending.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c6">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Great Elders]] are entities that will rise.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Great Elders]] could take over the world by taking over one person on the throne.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] can be opposed by the [[Guilds]] in times of emergency.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c13">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] are entities that could be freed by the [[Optasia]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]]' ultimate goal is to escape the world.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] cracked open the sky as an act of opportunity.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Great Elders]] are gathering their strength.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Guilds]] typically cooperate to maintain security on the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c20">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Great Elders]] are vulnerable in human form and can be killed.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The power of the [[Great Elders]] was required to hold open the sky.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c27">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“Our goal is to communicate with the Great Elders, to establish a common understanding so that we can benefit from just the tiniest fraction of their wisdom.”<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c22" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|"This is the only way for you to understand them, and to negotiate with them on an equal level."<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|“Our true enemies are not the Guild Heads. Our enemies are, as they have ever been, the Great Elders.”<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|“Or are they trying to give us no choice but to unify?”<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c2" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire groups]]
